kind.description: توسعات نصية أو قيم توليدية يطبقها المعالج المسبق قبل الترجمة.

identity.name: SDL_NODISCARD
identity.titleArabic: A macro to tag a function's return value as critical

summary.actualTranslationArabic: A macro to tag a function's return value as critical
summary.actualMeaningArabic: يمثل هذا الماكرو ماكرو SDL NODISCARD، أي توسعاً نصياً أو قيمة توليدية تؤثر في الكود قبل الترجمة.
summary.carriedValueArabic: الماكرو يوسع إلى #define SDL_NODISCARD [[nodiscard]] أو إلى تعبير مشتق منه، والقيمة الحقيقية هنا هي المعنى الذي يضيفه هذا التوسع إلى الكود.
summary.sourceDescription: A macro to tag a function's return value as critical.

remarks:
This is a hint to the compiler that a function's return value should
not be ignored.

If an NODISCARD function's return value is thrown away (the function
is called as if it returns void ), the compiler will issue a
warning.

While it's generally good practice to check return values for errors,
often times legitimate programs do not for good reasons. Be careful
about what functions are tagged as NODISCARD. It operates best when used
on a function that's failure is surprising and catastrophic; a good
example would be a program that checks the return values of all its file
write function calls but not the call to close the file, which it
assumes incorrectly never fails.

Function callers that want to throw away a NODISCARD return value can
call the function with a (void) cast, which informs the
compiler the act is intentional.

On compilers without nodiscard support, this is defined to
nothing.
